title: Mixed Precision
concept: Mixed precision speeds training and reduces memory use by doing much of the work with smaller number formats while keeping some sensitive values in higher precision for stability.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What makes GPUs such a strong fit for deep learning?

options.text: They handle many similar tensor calculations in parallel.
They replace the need for gradients.
They only work for tiny models.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Once you are already using GPU-friendly math, the next trick is often to store and compute some of those numbers more efficiently.

cards.title: Lighter containers, same suitcase
cards.body: Imagine packing the same trip into smaller bottles and thinner notebooks. You may fit more into the same bag and move faster through the airport. Mixed precision uses the same idea for model training: keep the important information, but use lighter numeric formats where possible.

cards.title: Not every number needs the same width
cards.body: Mixed precision means using lower-precision formats such as 16-bit numbers for much of the training work while keeping some sensitive quantities in higher precision, often 32-bit. The goal is to save memory and increase speed without making training unstable.

cards.title: Scratch notes vs. the official ledger
cards.body: A shop might use quick scratch notes while serving customers, but keep the official accounting ledger more carefully. Mixed precision is similar: much of the fast everyday computation can use lighter numbers, while a few values stay in a safer format so errors do not pile up.

cards.title: Fitting a larger batch on one GPU
cards.body: Suppose your model almost fills GPU memory with 32-bit training. Switching much of the work to smaller numeric formats can leave extra room, which may let you increase batch size or train a somewhat larger model on the same hardware.

cards.title: Lower precision is not the same as careless precision
cards.body: A common myth is that mixed precision means “just use tiny numbers everywhere and hope.” In practice, the “mixed” part matters. Engineers keep certain updates or accumulations in a safer format specifically because some parts of training are more numerically delicate than others.

cards.title: Full-width everywhere vs. lighter-by-default
cards.body: Mixed precision tries to keep the speed and memory gains without giving up stability.

left.text:
More memory per value
Often slower on supported hardware
Simple but heavier footprint

right.text:
Many operations use smaller formats
Often faster and more memory-efficient
Keeps some sensitive values safer

exercises.prompt: What is the main reason teams try mixed precision during training?

options.text: To reduce memory use and often gain speed while keeping critical parts stable.
To guarantee perfect accuracy on every dataset.
To remove the need for backpropagation.

exercises.explain: Correct. Mixed precision is a practical efficiency trick, not a replacement for training itself.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Mixed precision means every value in training must use the lowest-precision format available.
exercises.explain: False. The point is to mix formats so you get efficiency where possible and stability where needed.

exercises.sentence: Mixed precision keeps some sensitive calculations in higher ___.
exercises.explain: Exactly. Some parts of training are more sensitive, so they stay in higher precision.

exercises.prompt: Which situation best matches why mixed precision is useful?

options.text: A model almost fills GPU memory, so the team uses smaller numeric formats for much of training to fit more work on the same hardware.
A team wants to remove labels from a supervised dataset.
A team needs to replace matrix multiplication with handwritten rules.

exercises.explain: Right. Mixed precision is often about memory headroom and faster supported math on the same accelerator.

exercises.prompt: A training run on one GPU is stable but nearly out of memory, and the hardware supports fast lower-precision math. Which plan best matches mixed precision?

options.text: Use smaller number formats for much of the computation while keeping sensitive values in higher precision where needed.
Force every value into the smallest format with no safeguards at all.
Stop using gradients and update weights by hand.
Delete half the dataset so the GPU sees less information.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Mixed precision is a careful compromise: lighter math where it is safe, safer precision where training needs it.
